title: SPADE: Subsurface Permafrost Autonomous Discovery Engine
description: SPADE is the Subsurface Permafrost Autonomous Discovery Engine, an agentic data-preparation and model-simulation-setup agent for coupled surface-subsurface Earth system workflows. SPADE assembles permafrost observations and forcing datasets from CALM active-layer thickness, UAF GIPL borehole temperatures, GTN-P, the Alaska Permafrost Thaw Database (Webb et al. 2026), CommUnity near-Surface Permafrost (CUSP), NASA ABoVE, NGEE-Arctic, and additional Arctic data portals (ESS-DIVE, NSIDC, NCAR RDA, NOAA AWS Open Data, ASF Vertex, USGS NWIS, NOAA NWLON, PGC); harmonizes them into a unified pydantic schema with DuckDB provenance; supports site and regional simulation setup for ELM, ATS, ELM-FATES, ELM-MOSART, and related models; and includes a literature-review agent built on Semantic Scholar and the paper-search-mcp multi-platform backend. The initial source-document catalog focuses on Alaska and the pan-Arctic; SPADE's adapter library, schema, and provenance catalog are designed to transfer to other regions and other coupled surface-subsurface prediction systems.
